refactor.description: Refactor code for clarity and performance
refactor.template:
Refactor the following code. Explain what you changed and why.
$ARGUMENTS

explain.template:
Explain the following code in detail -- high-level purpose, data/control flow, design patterns, edge cases, complexity.
$ARGUMENTS

deps.template:
Audit project dependencies: outdated packages, security vulnerabilities, unused deps, duplicates, license issues.
$ARGUMENTS

clean.template:
Clean up build artifacts, caches, and ignored files. Ask before each destructive operation.
$ARGUMENTS

scaffold.template: Create a new $ARGUMENTS following the project's existing patterns, conventions, and file organization. Include test files.

learn.template:
Explain in depth (for someone with systems programming experience): $ARGUMENTS

Include: core concepts, how it works under the hood, use cases, pitfalls, code examples, resources.

diagram.template:
Generate a Mermaid diagram for: $ARGUMENTS

Choose the best diagram type (flowchart, sequence, class, ER, state, graph, gantt, gitGraph). Read relevant code first.

test.template:
Write comprehensive tests (happy path, errors, edge cases) for: $ARGUMENTS

Use the project's existing test framework and patterns. Run and fix failures.

commit.description: Stage changes and craft a commit message
commit.template:
Review changes, stage files, and craft a conventional commit message. Ask for confirmation before committing.
$ARGUMENTS

review.description: Review code for bugs, security, and best practices
review.template:
Review the following code thoroughly. Follow the review process in your agent instructions.
$ARGUMENTS

perf.template: Analyze for performance issues, measure, optimize, and re-measure: $ARGUMENTS

docker.template:
Analyze or create Docker configuration for: $ARGUMENTS

Follow Docker best practices from the devops agent instructions.

migrate.template: Plan and execute migration with rollback strategy: $ARGUMENTS

explore.template:
Explore and map: $ARGUMENTS

Provide a concise summary suitable for onboarding.

verify.template:
Independently verify recent changes: $ARGUMENTS

Report findings honestly with evidence.

architect.template:
Design architecture for: $ARGUMENTS

Follow the output structure in your agent instructions.

debug.description: Systematically find and fix a bug
debug.template:
Debug: $ARGUMENTS

Follow the debugging methodology in your agent instructions.

cp.template:
Solve: $ARGUMENTS

Read templates from ~/.config/opencode/scripts/cp/templates.md before implementing.

write.description: Write text in your personal style

pdf.template:
Read and analyse: $ARGUMENTS

Use `pdftotext "<path>" -` to extract text. Do NOT open .pdf files directly.

fix.description: Fix lint, type, and formatting errors
fix.template:
Fix all lint, type, and formatting errors: $ARGUMENTS

Fix at root cause, don't suppress. Re-run to verify.

plan.description: Think through a problem before implementing
plan.template:
Think through carefully before acting: $ARGUMENTS

Present the plan and ask before executing.

init.template:
Initialize a new project: $ARGUMENTS

Ask if language/framework unclear. Set up standard tooling, .gitignore, README. Verify it builds.

pr.template:
Create a PR for the current branch: $ARGUMENTS

Return the PR URL.

todo.description: Find TODOs, FIXMEs, HACKs in codebase
todo.template:
Find all TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments in source files (exclude node_modules, .git, build artifacts). Group by severity.
$ARGUMENTS

env.template:
Check dev environment health: tools, dependencies, configs, build.
$ARGUMENTS

mtg.description: Build, analyze, or optimize a Commander/EDH deck
mtg.template:
$ARGUMENTS

Follow the deck building workflow in your agent instructions. Use ALL data sources.
